draft.subject: Re: Careerspan perk for Marvin — ready to help with hiring
draft.body:
Hey Logan,

Thanks for signing up for the Careerspan demo through Marvin Ventures.

Saw you're actively hiring — that's exactly where we can help cut through the noise. You flagged screening as a pain point — we hear that a lot from founders.

As a Marvin member, you've got 3 months of role support covered — let's put it to work.

Ready to get started?
https://calendly.com/v-at-careerspan/30min

Best,
Vrijen S Attawar
CEO @ Careerspan
---
👉 Try Careerspan! (http://www.mycareerspan.com) and Follow us on LinkedIn! (https://www.linkedin.com/company/careerspan/)
✋ Let's connect on Twitter (https://mobile.twitter.com/VAttawar) or LinkedIn (http://www.linkedin.com/in/vrijenattawar)

submission.name: Logan Currie
pain_points: I'm spending too much time screening candidates (especially bad ones)
My best candidates seem to drop out of the process
My ATS isn't helping me narrow down candidates meaningfully

person.about:
Helping people build career IP in an AI-shaped world. 
15 years across 5 countries → startups, education, and the future of work.

🚀 Co-founder of Careerspan, where we’re reimagining career growth for the age of automation: dynamic skill mapping, narrative intelligence, and tools that help you outpace change.
 
🎓 Just finished up a masters at Harvard digging into how AI is reshaping work, learning, and self-advocacy.

🙌  Founding Member of Women Applying AI, volunteering in programming + community onboarding. 

🎯 Mission: Equip people to navigate work with confidence, clarity, and power.

When not geeking out about skills and the future of work, you'll find me:
- hunting for the best 面条 in Boston (suggestions welcome!)
- exploring the playgrounds of Cambridge with my kids
- reading many books ... both highbrow and lowbrow. 

💬 Always up for a good jam session on:
- AI in work + life 
- Zig-zag careers 
- All things skills and learning
- Travel (always)
person.headline:
Founder @Careerspan | Mom @home |
Reimagining the post-resume future of work

positionList.title: Director
positionList.description: Brought in to lead an international school after the founder repatriated. Designed and implemented a 21st-century learning curriculum along with extracurricular technology-focused programs. Led professional development for a staff from 5+ countries. Oversaw admissions, marketing, and operations.

positionList.title: Co Host
positionList.description: Co-hosted a podcast exploring the future of work, skills development, and career growth. Interviewed industry experts and education leaders to share insights with early-career professionals and educators.

positionList.title: Co-Founder
positionList.description: Built screen-free STEM toys to help kids learn through hands-on play. Ideated, designed, and tested prototypes for sustainable wooden toys to teach circuits, mechanics, and problem-solving (without screens, manuals, or a hundred pieces). Paused when COVID-19 lockdowns in China made shipping + manufacturing impossible. Anyone working in hardware/education? Reach out - would love to revive this but don’t have the bandwidth!

positionList.title: Business Development Manager & Head of Consulting
positionList.description: Worked closely with the CEO of a PE-backed education startup to design 'externships' connecting high school students with real-world work opportunities. Managed a team of 5, oversaw a large marketing budget and developed new programs for Singapore’s international schools. Despite early momentum, the company ultimately folded. I learned a lot about growth, product-market fit, and resilience.

positionList.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Head Coach
positionList.description: Co-founded a college and career consulting company helping students navigate applications, internships, and early career decisions through storytelling. Along with two cofounders, grew the business to serve clients across Asia, Europe, and the US. Experimented with scaling beyond 1:1 coaching, including launching a MOOC, but found personalization was key to success. Successfully exited the business.

positionList.title: Study Group Leader
positionList.description: Bringing together a select group of 50 graduate students from across Harvard to participate in speaker sessions, discussions + "share-outs" around topics at the intersection of the future of work and education.

experienceList.companyName: The Project on Workforce at Harvard

company.name: The Project on Workforce at Harvard

positionList.title: Mentor, Curriculum Developer, Test Prep Trainer
positionList.description: Joined as one of the first employees and witnessed the startup grow from a small team to hundreds of employees. Taught test prep, designed curriculum, and coached students while collaborating with the tech team to build an SAT learning app. Learned the fundamentals of personalized learning and the power of storytelling in education.

positionList.title: Co-Founder and COO
positionList.description: Building a career companion that helps people tell their career stories, stand out in the job market, and grow professionally. Leading operations, product strategy, and marketing while scaling partnerships with universities and bootcamps.

company.name: Careerspan
company.description: Careerspan offers personalized AI coaching to fill the career guidance void: helping users identify strengths, translate their skills into a compelling story on their applications, and navigate the job search process. Its goal is to enhance confidence, secure better jobs, and ensure equal opportunities for everyone.

relevant_content.title: Smart Friction Hiring Funnel Blurb
relevant_content.content: Turn inbound from a firehose into a filter. Careerspan adds smart friction to your hiring funnel that deters mass-appliers and reveals genuine fit—giving you higher-quality screens and more confident hiring decisions without adding recruiter headcount.

relevant_content.title: Hiring Signal Collapse Worldview
relevant_content.content:
---
created: 2025-12-05
last_edited: 2025-12-05
version: 2.0
---

# The Hiring Signal Collapse: A Worldview Synthesis

## The Core Thesis

**Inbound hiring is broken at a fundamental level.** Not broken in the sense of "needs optimization" but broken in the sense of "the underlying mechanism no longer produces valid signal." This is a structural failure, not a process inefficiency.

The apology I owe job seekers: 18 months ago, I encouraged people to tailor their applications. The advice was correct *in the world that existed then*. The world has changed. The advice no longer works—not because it was wrong in principle, but because the environment that made it work has collapsed.

---

## The Original Belief

**What I told people ~1.5 years ago:**

Tailored job applications work because of *pattern disruption*:
- In an environment where most applicants submit generic applications, personalization stands out
- Personalization carries two signals:
  1. **Implicit signal:** "I cared enough to invest time in this specific opportunity"
  2. **Explicit signal:** More relevant, focused information about fit
- The combination of attention + relevance = higher success rate

This was true. It worked. The advice helped people.

---

## What Changed: The Two-Sided Collapse

The system broke on both sides simultaneously.

### The Supply-Side Collapse

**AI made bad personalization free.**

Before generative AI, the cost structure was:
- Generic application: 5 minutes
- Tailored application: 30-60 minutes

That cost differential meant tailoring was a *reliable signal* of motivation and conscientiousness. Someone willing to invest an hour in your company probably cares.

After generative AI:
- Generic application: 30 seconds
- AI-generated "personalization": 30 seconds
- Genuine personalization: still 30-60 minutes

**The signal collapsed.** AI can generate infinite plausible-sounding tailored content with zero marginal cost. The output looks identical. The input is radically diff

# Smart Friction Hiring Funnel Blurb

Turn inbound from a firehose into a filter. Careerspan adds smart friction to your hiring funnel that deters mass-appliers and reveals genuine fit—giving you higher-quality screens and more confident hiring decisions without adding recruiter headcount.
